revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Isobe inamura-jinja]][[ja:礒部稲村神社]] {{Expand Japanese|礒部稲村神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Isobe inamura Shrine | native_name = 礒部稲村神社 | image = Isobe Inamura-jinja Worship hall.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Isobe inamura Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.368304|140.141064}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|http://isobe-inamurajinjya.com/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Isobe inamura Shrine'''|礒部稲村神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11481683|ltq=Q11481683|qid=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 11 ** P155: {{ill|Shizu Shrine|lt=Shizu Shrine|ja|静神社|qid=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039763|ltq=Q135039763|qid=Q135039763|1=Tachinono Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q990350|ltq=Q990350|qid=Q990350|1=Sakuragawa, Ibaraki}} * {{ill|Hitachi Province|lt=Hitachi Province|cs|Provincie Hitači|de|Provinz Hitachi|en|Hitachi Province|es|Provincia de Hitachi|fa|ولایت هیتاچی|fr|Province de Hitachi|gl|Provincia de Hitachi|hak|Hitachi-koet|id|Provinsi Hitachi|it|Hitachi (provincia)|ja|常陸国|ko|히타치국|lt|Hitačio žemė|nl|Hitachi (provincie)|pt|Província de Hitachi|ru|Хитати (провинция)|simple|Hitachi Province|sk|Hitači (provincia)|sr|Провинција Хитачи|tr|Hitachi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Хітаті|zh_min_nan|Hitati-kok|zh_yue|常陸國|zh|常陸國|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}} * {{ill|Q1075212|ltq=Q1075212|qid=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}} * {{ill|Q838663|ltq=Q838663|qid=Q838663|1=Hitachiōta, Ibaraki}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.368304|140.141064}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == official website (P856) == * http://isobe-inamurajinjya.com/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Isobe Inamura-jinja Worship hall.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181764 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Senior Sixth Rank|lt=Senior Sixth Rank|ja|正六位|ko|정6위|zh|正六位|qid=Q11545372|1=Senior Sixth Rank}} * {{ill|Junior Fifth Rank|lt=Junior Fifth Rank|ja|従五位|ko|종5위|zh|從五位|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}} == legal form (P1454) == * {{ill|religious corporation|lt=religious corporation|ja|宗教法人|qid=Q7311343|1=religious corporation}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == postal code (P281) == * 309-1457https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5050005010241https://www.houjin-bangou.nta.go.jp/download/zenken/index.html == Commons category (P373) == * Isobe inamura-jinja == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039762|ltq=Q135039762|qid=Q135039762|1=Inamurano Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県桜川市磯部字稲置779', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '茨城県常陸太田市天神林町', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Isobe inamura-jinja}} [[JA:礒部稲村神社]] {{wikidata link|Q65681696}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Isobe Inamura Shrine |画像 = [[File:Isobe Inamura-jinja Worship hall.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Sakuragawa|Q990350|qid=Q990350|1=Sakuragawa, Ibaraki}}Isobe, Aza Inaoki 779 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru-sume-Okami|1=UNKNOWN}} |神体 = Kaname-ishi (foundation stone) |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} disputed identification
former {{ill|lt=Gosha under modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} |創建 = {{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 40 ({{ill|lt=111|Q30640|qid=Q30640|1=AD 111}}), October |宮司 = |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Isobe Daimyojin |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 15|Q2982|qid=Q2982|1=November 15}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|label=Isobe
Inamura Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=250}} }} '''Isobe Inamura Shrine''' (いそべいなむらじんじゃ) is a shrine in {{ill|lt=Sakuragawa|Q990350|qid=Q990350|1=Sakuragawa, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, enshrining deities including {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru-sume-Okami|1=UNKNOWN}}. Its former name was Isobe Inamura-no-miya. It is a shikinaisha small shrine of Niihari District, {{ill|lt=Hitachi Province|Q1055353|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}Ibaraki Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines: [http://www.ibaraki-jinjacho.jp/ibaraki/kensei/jinja/03054.html Isobe Inamura Shrine], viewed July 10, 2019.. Centered on the shrine approach, part of the precinct and part of adjacent {{ill|lt=Isobe Sakuragawa Park|Q130458624|qid=Q130458624|1=Isobe Sakuragawa Park}} are designated as the national scenic site "Sakuragawa" (designated {{ill|lt=1924|Q2173|qid=Q2173|1=1924}}) and as the national natural monument "{{ill|lt=Cherry trees of Sakuragawa|Q55525177|qid=Q55525177|1=Cherry trees of Sakuragawa}}" (designated {{ill|lt=1974|Q2478|qid=Q2478|1=1974}}){{Cite journal |和書 |url=https://www.nat.museum.ibk.ed.jp/assets/data/materials/research/report/21/p081-90.pdf |author1=Toshio Katsuki|author2=Takeo Hinata|author3=Yuji Watanabe|author4=Ryo Isobe|author5=Takuya Yamakawa|title=Cherry varieties of nationally designated scenic/natural monument sites in Sakuragawa City, Ibaraki |journal=Bulletin of the Ibaraki Nature Museum |issue=21 |publisher=Ibaraki Nature Museum |year=2018 |pages=81-89}}. === History === Tradition says that in October of {{ill|lt=111|Q30640|qid=Q30640|1=AD 111}} ({{ill|lt=Emperor Keiko|Q329723|qid=Q329723|1=Emperor Keikō}} year 40), {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} transferred and enshrined {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iso-no-miya|1=UNKNOWN}}, an {{ill|lt=Ara-matsuri-no-miya|Q2859485|qid=Q2859485|1=Aramatsuri-no-Miya}} of {{ill|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|Q687168|qid=Q687168|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}. In {{ill|lt=849|Q23902|qid=Q23902|1=849}} ({{ill|lt=Kasho|Q1373157|qid=Q1373157|1=Kashō}} 2), during floods or drought, a rain-prayer rite was conducted at Kagami-ga-ike in the Yamaguchi settlement, Iwase Town. In {{ill|lt=939|Q23422|qid=Q23422|1=939}} ({{ill|lt=Tenkei|Q1327340|qid=Q1327340|1=Tengyō}} 2), {{ill|lt=Taira no Sadamori|Q3514057|qid=Q3514057|1=Taira no Sadamori}} prayed here for success in suppressing {{ill|lt=Taira no Masakado|Q1207652|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado}}. From the {{ill|lt=Heian period|Q193292|qid=Q193292|1=Heian period}} onward, the {{ill|lt=Onakatomi clan|Q11431853|qid=Q11431853|1=Ōnakatomi clan}}, who were priests of {{ill|lt=Katori Jingu|Q372380|qid=Q372380|1=Katori Jingū}} ({{ill|lt=Katori|Q858357|qid=Q858357|1=Katori, Chiba}}, {{ill|lt=Chiba Prefecture|Q80011|qid=Q80011|1=Chiba Prefecture}}), administered the shrine lands and sacred territoryAncient document of {{ill|lt=1245|Q1245|qid=Q1245|1=Enrico De Nicola}} ({{ill|lt=Kangen|Q1194187|qid=Q1194187|1=Kangen}} 3), held by {{ill|lt=Kashima Jingu|Q706499|qid=Q706499|1=Kashima Jingū}}. In {{ill|lt=1429|Q6326|qid=Q6326|1=1429}} ({{ill|lt=Eikyo|Q1193059|qid=Q1193059|1=Eikyō}} 1), {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Mochiuji|Q4805073|qid=Q4805073|1=Ashikaga Mochiuji}} of the {{ill|lt=Kamakura kubo|Q860777|qid=Q860777|1=Kantō kubō}} donated 25 kanmon (equivalent to 230 koku) as shrine land. In Eikyo 10 ({{ill|lt=1438|Q6377|qid=Q6377|1=1438}}), shrine priest Isobe Sukeyuki presented one volume of the hanami tale ''Sakurago Monogatari'' to Mochiuji. Seeing that story, the 6th shogun {{ill|lt=Ashikaga Yoshinori|Q239170|qid=Q239170|1=Ashikaga Yoshinori}} had {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Zeami Motokiyo|1=UNKNOWN}} compose the Noh play "{{ill|lt=Sakuragawa (Noh)|Q136744837|qid=Q136744837|lt=Sakuragawa|1=Sakuragawa}}"From the shrine noticeboard.. In {{ill|lt=1585|Q6736|qid=Q6736|1=1585}} ({{ill|lt=Tensho|Q1145058|qid=Q1145058|1=Tenshō (Momoyama period)}} 13), shrine lands were confiscated by the {{ill|lt=Yuki clan|Q2975399|qid=Q2975399|1=Yūki clan}} during Kanto warfare, but in the early {{ill|lt=Edo period|Q184963|qid=Q184963|1=Edo period}} it was protected by the {{ill|lt=Inoue clan|Q2975275|qid=Q2975275|1=Inoue clan}}, and {{ill|lt=Emperor Go-Mizunoo|Q320229|qid=Q320229|1=Emperor Go-Mizunoo}} granted a calligraphy plaque for {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isobe Daimyojin|1=UNKNOWN}} (still extant). In {{ill|lt=1696|Q7699|qid=Q7699|1=1696}} ({{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 9), {{ill|lt=Naito Masatomo|Q10514812|qid=Q10514812|1=Naitō Masatomo}}, lord of {{ill|lt=Akanuma Domain|Q10863285|qid=Q10863285|1=Akanuma Domain}} in {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|qid=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}, donated 15 koku 2 to 5 sho 2 go as shrine land. === Shrine names === Since ancient times the shrine has been called Isobe-no-miya, Isobe Inamura-no-miya, Sakuragawa Myojin, Isobe Daimyojin, Inamura Shrine, and Sakuragawa Isobe Inamura Shrine. Documents related to Hitachi Saidaisha Inada-hime Shrine (held at Hitachi Kokubunji Gokuraku-ji) list it as Isobe Inamura-no-miya in Niihari District, one of the 28 Hitachi shrines (Engishiki shrines). === Enshrined deities === ; Principal deities * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amateru-sume-Okami|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Takuhatachiji-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Seoritsuhime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Futotama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tamayori-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Tajikarao-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Tamahashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Uzume-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|Q4388732|qid=Q4388732|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Mikoto|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qid=Q2564508}} * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Yamato Takeru|1=Yamato Takeru|qid=Q461258}} === Subsidiary shrines === {{Empty section|date=August 2020}} === Festivals === {| class="wikitable" !Date!!Festival |- |January 1||New Year's Festival |- |Day of Setsubun in February||Setsubun Festival |- |First Horse Day in February||Hatsuuma Festival |- |February 11||National Foundation Day Festival |- |Second Saturday of April||Children's Growth Prayer Festival |- |April 15||Chinka Festival (Kinen Festival) |- |June 30||Nagoshi Great Purification |- |Before July 24||Gion Festival |- |November 23||Kenkoku Festival (rice-offering appraisal festival) |- |December 31||Year-end Great Purification |} === Access === * {{ill|lt=JR East|Q499071|qid=Q499071|lt=JR East|1=East Japan Railway Company}} {{ill|lt=Mito Line|Q302388|qid=Q302388|1=Mito Line}} {{ill|lt=Haguro Station (Ibaraki)|Q5638843|qid=Q5638843|lt=Haguro Station|1=Haguro Station (Ibaraki)}} (16 minutes on foot) === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === External links === * [http://isobe-inamurajinjya.com/index.html Sakuragawa Isobe Inamura Shrine] (official site) * [http://isobe-inamurajinjya.com/saishin.shintoku/gosaishin.html Sakuragawa Isobe Inamura Shrine] (deities page on official site) * [http://www.ibaraki-jinjacho.jp/ibaraki/kensei/jinja/03054.html Isobe Inamura Shrine] (Ibaraki Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) {{神道 横}} {{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Isobe inamura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Isobe inamura Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Sakuragawa, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Municipal History of Sakuragawa]]
